metrics.narrative: Jai Rastogi (CPO) has clearly articulated the qualitative pain (procurement 'bodies in spreadsheets,' cheese spend analysis taking 'two weeks' to 'months' to compile) and Zycus surfaced illustrative quantified impact on the 3 Jun 2026 demo (potential dairy consolidation savings of $126k-300k; 80% automation efficiency reported for paralegals; 600% ROI cited from other Zycus customers). None of these figures are yet Domino's-confirmed; they are Zycus estimates the POC is meant to validate against Domino's own data.

champion.narrative: Jai Rastogi (Group CPO, ex-Thai Union) is a strong, active champion: he initiated the relationship at a conference, personally sequenced the demo and POC plan, is 'coaching' his internal team ahead of stakeholder reviews, brought in the group head of reporting & analytics and a finance lead unprompted for buy-in, and on 3 Jul 2026 proactively expanded the evaluation to the relevant stakeholder (Dani de Jong, Michael Gengenbach, Maria Oehler). He explicitly said Zycus has 'a front run' given no formal market process — advocacy with real access and initiative, not just politeness.

competition.narrative: This is a sole-source evaluation: Jai stated plainly on 29 May 2026, 'I haven't gone out to market to anyone.' Coupa, SAP Ariba, and iValua surface only as tools Jai personally implemented in prior roles (Thai Union and elsewhere) — reference points for his own expertise, not active bids against Zycus at Domino's. The wedge is uncontested access plus the agentic AI (Merlin/ANA) story landing well with a technology-forward buyer; the risk is not a rival but the POC failing to produce a compelling enough proof point to convert the in-principle intent into a signed award.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is extensively and specifically articulated by Jai across two calls: procurement maturity is 'exceptionally low... bodies in spreadsheets,' there is 'no common taxonomy' across disparate ERPs, cheese spend analysis alone takes weeks to compile, indirect spend (travel, marketing, technology) is 'completely unmanaged,' and there is 'no CLM system... significant contract leakage.' The 3 Jul 2026 Europe call added that 12-13 markets run different ERP setups, some with no ERP at all, and Germany is still in D365 hypercare — compounding the same underlying data-fragmentation pain across regions. Jai is the clear pain owner driving the transformation mandate.

sources: Avoma calls (29 May, 3 Jun, 3 Jul 2026) — absence of paper-process discussion
paper_process.narrative: No MSA, Order Form, SOW, or InfoSec review has been discussed on any read call, which is expected and normal at Formal Evaluation stage ahead of a POC-driven in-principle award. Visibility on contracting mechanics (procurement channel, legal review, InfoSec questionnaire) would only be created once the POC concludes and Domino's moves toward the in-principle award Jai referenced.

economic_buyer.narrative: Lynn Carruthers (CFO) is named the Executive Sponsor / Economic Buyer on the Opportunity Contact Role, and the 3 Jun 2026 call notes record that 'Head of Finance will review POC outcomes for integration decision' and a finance head is expected to join the POC discussion for budget approval. However, Lynn Carruthers has not personally appeared on any read call as of 8 Jul 2026 — Jai is coaching internal stakeholders and building the ROI case on her behalf rather than her engaging directly.

decision_process.narrative: The process the buyer is running is demo -> data-driven POC -> internal signoff -> 'in-principal agreement to award' (per Next_Step__c, 29 May 2026 entry), with Jai personally sequencing which stakeholders join at each stage (finance and technology procurement first, legal carved out separately for CLM, and Domino's Europe stakeholders pulled in on 3 Jul 2026 to widen scope). The approval chain past Jai (CFO sign-off, IT alignment) has not yet been walked through on a call.

decision_criteria.narrative: Jai laid out explicit priorities on 29 May 2026: first, spend visibility/analytics on Domino's own disparate data; second, a CLM to centralize contracts and cut leakage; and an agentic AI (Merlin) overlay throughout. The 3 Jul 2026 Europe call narrowed this into a concrete POC criterion set: iAnalyze (spend analysis) plus ANA (autonomous negotiation) proving value on 3 months of indirect spend data before any broader rollout.

items.name: POC scope & platform overview call
items.summary: Zycus (Luke Dougherty, Shirley Islam) walked Domino's (Jai Rastogi, Michael Gengenbach, Maria Oehler, Antje Radüchel-Harders, Dani de Jong) through the platform, agreeing the POC will start with iAnalyze spend analysis before moving to Anna for tactical low-dollar negotiations; three months of indirect spend data was requested as the initial input, with Zycus handling normalization pending Domino's validation.

items.name: Shirley's post-meeting recap and materials
items.summary: Shirley Islam sent Domino's the POC scope materials and the three templates reviewed on the call (iAnalyze first, Anna to follow), asking for an initial data cut on chosen indirect categories to begin iterative normalization; she also flagged the Anna negotiation export will follow on Monday, leaving Domino's to respond with data and category scope.

items.name: Scheduling thread that set up the call
items.summary: Included in the thread is the prior back-and-forth where Michael Gengenbach (new European lead, unable to attend the original demo) proposed Friday 8.30-9.30 CET as an alternative since he's on holiday from the following week, which Zycus accommodated — explaining why the meeting landed on this date.

items.name: Duplicate meeting record with same scheduling email
items.summary: A second logged instance of the same scheduling thread/event description, reflecting calendar/system logging of the rescheduled call rather than new substantive activity.

day_summary.overall: The rescheduled platform overview finally happened, with Zycus walking Domino's European team (Jai Rastogi, Michael Gengenbach, Maria Oehler and others) through the POC scope, confirming it will start with iAnalyze spend insights before layering in Anna for tactical negotiations. Zycus followed up same-day with the promised templates and materials, and the ball is now in Domino's court to check what indirect spend data (Navision/ERP extracts) they can actually pull and confirm scope, likely narrowed to a category like packaging.

Jai flagged 6 Jul 'some concerns about executing POC with disparate DPE resources'. 3 Jul call confirmed data extraction is uncertain and Domino's must check Navision/ERP formats and advise feasibility. If data pull stalls or is too messy for Zycus to normalize, POC cannot run and momentum dies.

CFO (Lynn Carruthers, Economic Buyer) has not directly engaged and is passive. Jai Rastogi (CPO) is coaching finance stakeholders to build the internal ROI case via the POC, but CFO approval is not locked. If POC results are thin or the ROI narrative is weak, CFO may defer investment given cost-pressure context (share price fell, transformation funding is tight). Jai stated 'if we see some real tangible results using our data... there'll be a lot of momentum' — the inverse is also true: no tangible results = no momentum.

ANA (autonomous negotiation agent) French-language support unconfirmed. Maria Oehler (Domino's Germany) asked 3 Jul whether ANA can communicate with French vendors in French; Zycus answer pending. If ANA is English-only, POC scope may need to narrow to English-speaking markets (Australia, some Europe) or exclude certain suppliers, which could reduce demonstrated savings and weaken the ROI case.

If data delivery slips 2-3 weeks, POC runs in late Jul, results land early Aug, leaving minimal time for CFO approval, contracting, legal/InfoSec review, and signature by 28 Aug. Close date may slip to Q3/Q4 2026 if POC or contracting takes longer than planned.

POC scope has both expanded (AU to Germany/Netherlands) and needs narrowing per the 3 Jul 2026 recommendation to a single starter category (e.g. packaging) - locking this now will speed a demonstrable win ahead of the 28 Aug close date.

Bring Lynn Carruthers into POC progress reviews now, not after results land: Jai is building the internal case alone, and a cost-pressured CFO (Domino's share price fell $161 to $16.68) will need direct visibility to release budget.

Protect the sole-source position by moving fast: no formal RFP is running and Jai has explicitly ruled out shopping the market (29 May 2026); a slow POC creates room for internal governance to force a competitive process.
Domino's spend data spans 12-13 disparate ERPs with no common taxonomy; offering hands-on Zycus data-normalization help now is the single highest-leverage move to get the POC (and the win) unstuck.

The CFO holds the budget but has never engaged directly; getting her into an early POC results review protects against SF's own flagged risk that internal politics could reopen the field to Coupa or iValua.

Jai Rastogi is a strong, multi-threading champion who brought in Domino's Europe unprompted; keep him tightly coached with a realistic POC timeline and a sharp ROI story to sustain the momentum he is building.

Sole-source status is fragile until the POC lands tangible results: Jai warned 'if we see some real tangible results... there'll be a lot of momentum' (29 May 2026) — prioritize a fast, narrow POC (packaging category, per the 3 Jul 2026 Europe suggestion) over a broad multi-market pull to protect that momentum.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Domino's is a well-qualified but very early deal: strong, specific pain and an engaged procurement sponsor, but no proof-of-concept has launched yet, the economic buyer has not personally engaged, and the deal sits well before the milestones (validation, commercials, negotiation) that would make an Aug 2026 close credible.

deal_risk.text: Wayne McMahon (CIO, Influencer) has not engaged on any read call despite ERP integration and data feasibility being central to the current POC blocker.

The POC depends on normalizing spend data across 12-13 disparate market ERPs (Navision, D365, various local systems) with no common taxonomy - a real execution risk Jai Rastogi himself flagged on 6 Jul 2026.

win_position.text: Jai Rastogi (CPO) told Zycus directly on 29 May 2026 that Domino's has not gone to market for this evaluation, giving Zycus a first-mover position ahead of any formal competitive process.
Pain is sharply and specifically defined (spreadsheet-bound procurement, no CLM, contract leakage, a two-week cheese-spend roll-up) rather than generic, which supports a credible POC business case.
Lynn Carruthers (CFO, Executive Sponsor) has not appeared on any read call; the deal currently routes entirely through Jai Rastogi with no direct economic-buyer engagement.
No proof-of-concept has launched as of the 6 Jul 2026 Next Step update, and the data-extraction step it depends on is not yet confirmed as feasible across Domino's disparate ERPs.

deal_momentum.text: A verified two-way buyer touch (email reply) was logged 2026-07-02, and a full call with an expanded Domino's Europe group ran 2026-07-03, both within the last 7-8 days.
The core forward step - Domino's delivering usable spend-data extracts - is the same blocker named in the 22 Jun and 6 Jul 2026 Next Step entries, indicating it has not moved for roughly two weeks.
